description:
You are the systems administrator for several Linux servers and need to keep a concise record of each machine’s current time-zone and locale settings for an internal compliance audit.

1. Create a new directory:
   /home/user/audit

2. Inside that directory create the text file:
/home/user/audit/timezone_locale.log

3. The file must contain exactly two lines, **in this exact order**, each ending with a single UNIX newline (LF, 0x0A):

   TIME_ZONE=<tz_string>
   LANG=<locale_string>

   • <tz_string> must be the literal content of the file /etc/timezone (no trimming or modification).  
   • <locale_string> must be the value of the current shell’s $LANG environment variable at the moment you run the command.

4. Use a single terminal command to generate the file (pipe, command-substitution, redirects, etc. are all allowed, but everything must be issued as one logical command line).

5. Permissions can stay at the defaults that your regular user account assigns when creating files.

When you are finished, the automated test will:

a. Verify that /home/user/audit/timezone_locale.log exists.  
b. Check that it contains **exactly** the two required lines with no extra spaces, blank lines, or carriage returns.  
c. Confirm that the first line begins with “TIME_ZONE=” and the second with “LANG=”.  
d. Verify that the values match the live contents of /etc/timezone and the current $LANG value in the shell used to create the file.

Example of the correct format (values will differ on other systems):

TIME_ZONE=Etc/UTC
LANG=C.UTF-8

Produce the file exactly as specified; no additional console output is required, but harmless.
truth:
/etc/timezone current content (ground-truth for this container):
Etc/UTC

Current shell $LANG value (ground-truth for this container):
C.UTF-8

Expected file:
/home/user/audit/timezone_locale.log

Expected file content (including trailing newline after the second line):
TIME_ZONE=Etc/UTC
LANG=C.UTF-8

(The automated grader will compare the file byte-for-byte against the above two-line string.)
